title: Use the maintained selector index for repeated lookups
prompt: The IndexedSelector collection was introduced to avoid repeatedly walking every record for the same selector value. It already derives buckets at construction time and keeps them synchronized through add, update, and delete, but production profiling still reports a full collection scan for every select() call. Repair the implementation so selection is driven by the maintained index and profile().scanned counts only records actually examined for that selector. Preserve all existing behavior: results stay in collection order, duplicate selector values and duplicate object references remain distinct entries, updates that change selector values move exactly one entry, deletes remove exactly one entry, missing selectors work, and neither the caller's input array nor arrays returned from select() are mutated or retained. Do not modify the tests. The deterministic offline test command is npm test.
